scenario: agent-skills adapter recognize(component, file) for every file in tests/fixtures/bundles/agent-skills/ — standalone SKILL.md packages. Emits type=skill; item = the package directory. Recognition does NOT gate on validity (invalid skills are still recognized; violations surface in validate). SPECIFICATION golden.
adapterStatus: NOT IMPLEMENTED — spec-authored target the future agent-skills adapter must reproduce (future Chunk-2 format-adapter work item). SKILL.md codec shared with claude/opencode as functions (spec §8).
specSource: docs/design/akm-0.9.0-bundle-adapter-spec.md §7 (agent-skills adapter row: 'standalone SKILL.md packages'), §6 (skill row: SKILL.md → skill, item = the dir), §4.5 (Agent Skills contract), §1.2 (looksLikeRoot: root SKILL.md).
realWorldSource: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/agent-skills/overview — SKILL.md package structure; required fields name + description; name max 64, lowercase letters/numbers/hyphens, no XML tags, no reserved words 'anthropic'/'claude'; description non-empty max 1024; progressive disclosure metadata/instructions/resources
https://github.com/anthropics/skills — the reference skills repository (a collection of SKILL.md packages)
notes: SPECIFICATION golden — no agent-skills adapter exists in src/. The bundle models a COLLECTION of skill packages (like github.com/anthropics/skills): each */SKILL.md is one skill item whose conceptId is the package DIRECTORY; bundled resources under the package (reference/FORMS.md) are part of the item, not standalone concepts.

RECOGNITION ≠ VALIDATION: the Data_Analysis and overlong-summary skills are RECOGNIZED as type=skill even though they violate Agent Skills hard rules — recognition emits the IndexDocument, and validate()/lint surfaces the violations (see lint.json). This mirrors how the okf adapter recognizes content and defers leniency to validate.
The name field (frontmatter) is projected to IndexDocument.name; for the invalid skills the recognized name is the raw (invalid) value, so the violation is inspectable downstream.

pdf-processing/SKILL.md.name: pdf-processing
pdf-processing/SKILL.md.description: Extract text and tables from PDF files, fill forms, and merge documents. Use when working with PDF files or when the user mentions PDFs, forms, or document extraction.
pdf-processing/SKILL.md.derivation: SKILL.md → skill; item = the parent directory pdf-processing (conceptId, NOT .../SKILL.md). Conformant.

pdf-processing/reference/FORMS.md.reason: Bundled Level-3 resource inside the pdf-processing skill package — part of the skill item, not a standalone concept (item-scoped incrementality, spec §4).

Data_Analysis/SKILL.md.name: Data_Analysis
Data_Analysis/SKILL.md.derivation: SKILL.md → skill. RECOGNIZED despite the invalid name; the charset violation is reported by validate() (see lint.json), not by recognize().

overlong-summary/SKILL.md.name: overlong-summary
overlong-summary/SKILL.md.derivation: SKILL.md → skill. Name is valid; the over-length description is reported by validate() (see lint.json).
